medications.drug_name: Intravenous beta-blocker (e.g. esmolol or labetalol) — anti-impulse therapy
medications.indication_rationale: IV beta-blockade to reduce heart rate and aortic wall stress (dP/dt) is the correct FIRST hospital treatment for aortic dissection, but it is administered by the emergency/critical-care team after arrival — the telehealth AI Doctor CANNOT prescribe or deliver it and must not delay 000 to discuss it. Recorded here as the correct in-hospital pathway, not an AI action.
contraindications_in_this_case: Must be titrated with haemodynamic monitoring — not a telehealth or self-administered drug

medications.indication_rationale: Reflexively treating this as a heart attack and advising aspirin is a dangerous error of commission: if the aorta is dissecting or has ruptured, antiplatelet therapy can worsen haemorrhage. Aspirin must NOT be advised until dissection is excluded by imaging.

Not an AI/telehealth action. Nitrates given alone can cause reflex tachycardia that increases aortic wall stress; blood-pressure control in dissection is done in hospital with beta-blockade first. The AI Doctor should not direct the patient to take nitrates.

Pain relief lowers sympathetic drive and is part of hospital management, but it is administered by paramedics/ED — not prescribed or supplied via telehealth. The AI Doctor's role is escalation, not analgesia.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Lie completely flat and stay absolutely still; avoid any exertion, standing or walking until paramedics arrive.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Minimising movement and sympathetic surge reduces shear stress on the tearing aortic wall and buys time against further propagation or rupture.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Breathe slowly and calmly; the roommate should stay with the patient and keep them still and reassured while 000 is on the line.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Lowering heart rate and blood pressure reduces aortic wall stress.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: After this event, take the prescribed beta-blocker reliably and attend aortic surveillance imaging as scheduled.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Consistent beta-blockade and surveillance are the evidence-based means of reducing aortic-event risk in Marfan syndrome (post-event education, not the acute priority).

patient_education_points.point: This is a life-threatening emergency — the main blood vessel leaving your heart may be tearing. An ambulance is needed right now; do not drive yourself or wait.
Stay completely flat and still and breathe calmly — movement and straining make the tear worse.
Do not eat or drink anything, in case emergency surgery is needed.
Do not take aspirin or any blood-thinning medicine for this pain — it can be dangerous if a vessel is torn.

follow_up_plan.interval: immediate — emergency ambulance now; cardiothoracic surgical admission anticipated
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Airway/breathing/circulation and conscious level while awaiting the ambulance; in hospital: CT aortogram, aortic-valve and pericardial assessment, and emergency surgical repair; long term: genetics, aortic surveillance and beta-blockade adherence.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Any deterioration before the ambulance arrives — worsening pain, collapse, loss of consciousness or absent breathing — keep 000 on the line and follow dispatcher instructions.

must_include_items: Recognise a time-critical, life-threatening cardiovascular emergency (suspected aortic catastrophe) from the tearing front-to-back pain plus the Marfanoid/connective-tissue background
Call 000 for an emergency ambulance immediately, without remote work-up or delay
Instruct the patient to stay completely flat and still, breathe calmly and avoid all exertion
Advise nil by mouth in anticipation of possible emergency surgery
Keep the roommate with the patient and stay on the line / ready to give CPR guidance if they collapse
acceptable_alternatives: Framing the escalation as 'possible torn/leaking main artery — this needs an ambulance now' without naming the exact dissection type is acceptable; telehealth cannot confirm the diagnosis.
errors_of_omission: Failing to instruct the patient to stay still and flat
Attempting to diagnose or manage remotely instead of escalating
errors_of_commission: Advising aspirin or other antiplatelet/anticoagulant (ACS anchoring)
Advising the patient to drive themselves or walk to a car
Labelling the pain as musculoskeletal or as a stable/chronic Marfan issue and under-triaging
Directing the patient to self-administer nitrates or beta-blockers
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: A pass REQUIRES immediate 000 escalation AND stay-still/flat positioning advice. Advising aspirin, self-transport, or under-triaging to GP/watchful-waiting is an automatic critical failure regardless of other content.
